name: produce-the-phase-2-prod-20260513-1821
idea: Produce the Phase 2 prod cutover runbook at docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md for the live-update editor redesign (per the linked v6 redesign doc). Phase 1 will deliver the new editor in dev with publishing OFF; Phase 2 enables publishing, runs new + old editors side-by-side in prod for 24-48h, monitors for partial-publish failures via topics.publication_status='partial', renames old tables to _legacy_*, and schedules deletion in 2-4 weeks. The runbook must specify: exact publishing-flag-flip procedure with pre-flip preconditions, partial-publish detection SQL (the queries oncall would run), rollback procedure (how to flip the runner back to the legacy editor without rewriting state), the comparison-with-shadow-mode rubric (what specific signals from the Phase-1 shadow-mode logs mean cutover is safe versus 'go back and tune'), the table-rename migration SQL with correct ordering and constraint handling, and a decision tree for the 24-48h watch period — what observable symptom triggers what action (abort, hot-fix, accept). Assume Phase 1 lands per spec; deviations get a 'consult the doc' escape hatch, not detailed branching. The runbook is the operator's source of truth during cutover.

overrides.reason: hermes backend not installed; switching to all-codex profile

latest_failure.suggested_action: Inspect phase output and resume from the failed phase.
